set_width_rule
set_width_rule
expression_of_.width
-obj shapeObj
-name "ruleName"
[-direction {all | vertical | horizontal | corner_distance}]
[-type {simple |concave | jog | incorner}]
[-description string]
[-severity string]
[{-exception_triggering_base_cell shapeObj | -triggering_base_cell shapeObj}
[-triggering_spacing float]
[-effective_extension float]]

Specifies width constraints for specific object(s).


Parameters

 -description string                   Specifies the description of this rule.
 -direction {all | vertical | horizontal | corner_distance}

                                       Specifies the check direction.




Last Updated in September 2025                                    2316                  Product Version 23.16
                                       Innovus Stylus Common UI User Guide
                                    Syntax and Scripts--Supported UFC Commands



 -effective_extension float      Specifies the effective extension value relative to the object or
                                 exception object. The extension direction is perpendicular to the
                                 direction specified by the -direction parameter.
                                 Default: 0
                                 The -effective_extension parameter must be used with the –
                                 exception_triggering_base_cell or -triggering_base_cell
                                 parameters at the same time. The following diagram shows the
                                 relationship between them.
                                 For example:
                                 -exception_triggering_base_cell cell_A -triggering_spacing S
                                 -effective_extension E




 -exception_triggering_base_cell shapeObj

                                 Specifies the base cell name that triggers the width check exception.

 expression_of_.width            Specifies the dimension expression of .width to check for the valid
                                 width.

 -name "ruleName"                Specifies the rule name.

 -obj shapeObj                   Specifies the target object to check against this rule.

 -severity string                Specifies the severity.

 -                               Specifies the base cell name that triggers the width check.
 triggering_base_cell shapeObj




Last Updated in September 2025                              2317                                         Product Version 23.16
                                           Innovus Stylus Common UI User Guide
                                        Syntax and Scripts--Supported UFC Commands



 -triggering_spacing float           Specifies the maximum spacing from the excepted or included width
                                     to the triggering base cell. The spacing direction is the same as the
                                     direction specified by the -direction parameter.
                                     Note: It must be used with the –exception_triggering_base_cell or
                                     -triggering_base_cell parameters at the same time.

                                     Default: 0
                                     The -triggering_spacing parameter must be used with the –
                                     exception_triggering_base_cell or -triggering_base_cell
                                     parameters at the same time. The following diagram shows the
                                     relationship between them.
                                     For example:
                                     -triggering_base_cell cell_A -triggering_spacing S -
                                     effective_extension E




 -type {simple | concave | jog | incorner}

                                     Specifies the type of width to be checked.




Understanding width types and direction

The following image can be used as a quick reference for understanding the width direction and type.




Last Updated in September 2025                                2318                                           Product Version 23.16
                                             Innovus Stylus Common UI User Guide
                                          Syntax and Scripts--Supported UFC Commands




Where,

 A   Simple width in vertical direction. (Width between
     two horizontal edge directly project to each other)

 B   Simple width in horizontal direction.

 C   In-corner width in vertical direction. (Width
     between two 270-degree corners)

 D   In-corner width in horizontal direction.

 E   Jog width in vertical direction. (Width between
     one 90-degree corner and one 270-degree
     corners)

 F   Jog width in horizontal direction.

 G   Concave width in vertical direction. (Width
     between two horizontal edges PRL = 0)

 H   Concave width in horizontal direction.


Example

     The following command specifies the rule name, the expression of .width to check for the valid width and the type, direction
     and description.
     set_width_rule -name "WIDTH.CORE.1" -obj [available_sites] -type simple -direction horizontal {on_grid
     (.width-0.66, 1.32)} \
     -description {The simple width of available sites in horizontal direction must be 1.32*n+0.66.}


     Violations of width rule:




Last Updated in September 2025                                2319                                           Product Version 23.16
                                          Innovus Stylus Common UI User Guide
                                       Syntax and Scripts--Supported UFC Commands




     set_width_rule -name "DMTB.X2" -obj [available_sites] -type simple -direction vertical -
     exception_triggering_base_cell [base_cells SRAM}] \
         -effective_extension    0.153 {on_grid(.width-0.13,0.4)} \
         -description {set_width_constraint -label "DMTB.X2" -class useful_site -width_type simple -direction
     vertical -except_project_lib SRAM -project_extension 0.153 -grid 0.4 -offset 0.13}

     set_width_rule -name "DMTB.X3" -obj [available_sites] -type simple -direction vertical {on_grid(.width-
     0.15,0.4)} \
          -description {set_width_constraint -label "DMTB.X3" -class useful_site -width_type simple -direction
     vertical -grid 0.4 -offset 0.15}

     set_width_rule -name "DMTB.X4" -obj [available_sites] -type simple -direction horizontal -
     exception_triggering_base_cell [base_cells SRAM] {on_grid(.width-0.2,0.1)} \
          -description {set_width_constraint -label "DMTB.X4" -class useful_site -width_type simple -direction
     vertical -except_project_lib SRAM -grid 0.1 -offset 0.2}

     set_width_rule -name "DMTB.X5" -obj [available_sites] -type simple -direction horizontal {on_grid(.width-
     0.12,0.1)} \
          -description {set_width_constraint -label "DMTB.X5" -class useful_site -width_type simple -direction
     vertical -except_project_lib SRAM -grid 0.1 -offset 0.12}

     The following command uses the -exception_triggering_base_cell, -effective_extension and -triggering_spacing width rules:
     set_width_rule -name "DMTB.X6" -obj [available_sites] -type simple -direction vertical -
     exception_triggering_base_cell [base_cells Macro}] \
          -effective_extension 0.15 -triggering_spacing 0.16 {on_grid(.width-3.2,0.4)} \
          -description {set_width_constraint -label "MTB.X6" -class useful_site -width_type simple -direction
     vertical -except_project_lib Macro -project_extension 0.15 -project_spacing 0.16 -grid 0.4 -offset 3.2}


     Here, for DMTB.X6, the spacing between the below macro and available_sites is 0.175 which is larger than 0.16 specified by
     -triggering_spacing. So the available_sites area with extension 0.15 specified by -effective_extension will be checked:

set_density_rule
set_density_rule
-name "ruleName"
-obj string
-ref string
-mode {area | amount}
-cut_ref {all| none | proportion}
-window {global | local}
[-window_pitch {x y}]
[-window_offset {x y}]


Specifies the object density of the specified checked window.


Parameters

 -cut_ref {all| none | proportion}




Last Updated in September 2025                                  2322                 Product Version 23.16
                                           Innovus Stylus Common UI User Guide
                                        Syntax and Scripts--Supported UFC Commands



                    Specifies how to count the -ref object when it is partially inside the
                    -obj object.

                       all: The tool fully removes the -ref object from inside the -obj
                       object.

                       none: The tool takes the -ref object inside the -obj object.

                       proportion: The tool takes the -ref object inside the -obj
                       object on the real proportion.

                    For example, the following diagram shows how the density is
                    calculated if the -ref object is partially inside the -obj object.




 -mode {area |      Specifies the mode of density calculation.
 amount}
                       area: Check the area density.

                       amount: Check the number of objects.

 -                  Specifies the rule name.
 name "ruleName"

 -obj string        Specifies the object that contains the object for which the density is
                    to be measured.




Last Updated in September 2025                                 2323                          Product Version 23.16
                                           Innovus Stylus Common UI User Guide
                                        Syntax and Scripts--Supported UFC Commands



 -ref string        Specifies the object to be measured.
                    Note: The set_density_rule command checks the density of
                    object specified by option -ref in the object specified by option -
                    obj.

 -window {global    Specifies the region of the checked window that is used for density
 | local}           calculation.
                       global: The tool checks density based on real areas or amounts
                       of objects.




                       local: The tool checks the density based on windows which are
                       made up of option -window_offset {x y} and -
                       window_pitch {x y}. The tool cuts the die area into many areas
                       with specified window_pitch_x and window_pitch_y from the
                       origin of the die boundary. If the -window_offset option is
                       specified, all the windows will move with the specified offset
                       value.




Last Updated in September 2025                                2324                        Product Version 23.16
                                          Innovus Stylus Common UI User Guide
                                       Syntax and Scripts--Supported UFC Commands



 -window_pitch      Specifies the pitch of the checked window. This option must be
 {x y}              specified with the -window local option at the same time.
                    The tool checks the windows with the specified window pitch.




 -window_offset     Specifies the offset of the checked window. This option must be
 {x y}              specified with the -window local option at the same time.
                    The tool checks the windows with the specified window offset.




Last Updated in September 2025                               2325                     Product Version 23.16
                                         Innovus Stylus Common UI User Guide
                                      Syntax and Scripts--Supported UFC Commands



Example

     set_density_rule -name “BUMP.DENSITY” -obj [design_boundary] -ref [base_cells BUMPCELL_ROUND] \
     -mode amount -cut_ref none -window local -window_pitch {500 500} -window_offset {500 500} {.density <= 15} \
     -description {set_density_constraint -label "BUMP.DENSITY" -class1 design -lib_cell2 BUMPCELL_ROUND \
     -numerator amount -cut_method keep -density_method local_window -window_size {500 500} -window_step {500 500}
     -max 15}


     The following figure shows that there are 16 bumps when we set -cut_ref as none in the windows. These are marked as
     violations.
